## Highly pathogenic Avian Influenza in Belgium

Situation and measures in place

15-16 December 2025

<!-- image -->

## Summary of the outbreaks in Belgium since 01/10/2025

| Place          | Strain    | Date       | Type holding                       |
|----------------|-----------|------------|------------------------------------|
| Welkenraedt    | HPAI H5N1 | 20/10/2025 | Captive birds: hobby holder        |
| Houthulst      | HPAI H5N1 | 22/10/2025 | Poultry: broilers and meat turkeys |
| Weelde         | HPAI H5N1 | 25/10/2025 | Poultry: chicken breeders          |
| Dilsen-Stokkem | HPAI H5N1 | 20/11/2025 | Poultry: laying hens               |
| Kinrooi        | HPAI H5N1 | 22/11/2025 | Poultry: rearing laying hens       |
| Pelt (1)       | HPAI H5N1 | 23/11/2025 | Poultry: laying hens               |
| Pelt (2)       | HPAI H5N1 | 24/11/2025 | Poultry: broilers                  |
| Gembloux       | HPAI H5N1 | 23/11/2025 | Poultry: organic broilers          |
| La Bruyère (1) | HPAI H5N1 | 23/11/2025 | Poultry: chicken breeders          |
| La Bruyère (2) | HPAI H5N1 | 25/11/2025 | Poultry: meat ducks                |
| Oudenaarde     | HPAI H5N1 | 03/12/2025 | Captive birds: hobby holder        |

## Zones and outbreaks in Belgium

- Netherlands: 2 H5N1 outbreaks near the Belgian border
- 2 surveillances zones delimited on Belgium territory Steenbergen A17 Breda A27 Tilburg A2 Roosendaal ASa AS
- Belgium: 11 H5N1 outbreaks
- Captive birds: 2 outbreaks : closed
- Poultry:
- Houthulst and Ravel : Closed outbreaks and zones were lifted
- Dilsen-Stokkem, Kinrooi, Pelt, Gembloux and La Bruyère: zones still delimited

## Outbreaks of HPAI H5N1 in Captive birds Welkenraedt and Oudenaarde

<!-- image -->

[Welkenraedt, Oudenaerde]

|                      | Welkenraedt                                                                                        | Oudenaarde                                                                                                            |
|----------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Province             | Liege                                                                                              | East Flanders                                                                                                         |
| Establishement       | Hobby holder with 30 captive birds (20 hens, 2 turkeys, 2 swans and 6 cygnets)                     | Hobby holder with 20 captive birds (2 hens, 1 rooster, 2 peacocks, 11 geese including 7 barnacle, 4 common shelducks) |
| Date of confirmation | 20 th October 2025                                                                                 | 3 rd December 2025                                                                                                    |
| Symptomes/Mortality  | High mortality                                                                                     | High mortality and general weakness                                                                                   |
| Source               | Contact with wild birds, which regularly frequented the meadow where the captive birds were raised | Contact with wild birds which frequented a pond near the site where the captive birds were raised                     |

<!-- image -->

## Outbreaks of HPAI H5N1 in Poultry Houthulst

- o Houthulst: Province of West Flanders
- o Commercial farm with 20,354 meat turkeys and 46,756 broilers
- o Confirmation outbreak: 22 nd October 2025 in Turkeys
- o Broilers negative
- o increased mortality in turkeys
- o Source : most likely wild birds

o

## 2 nd farm on the same street

- o Preventive culling and sampling -> HPAI Negative
- o Commercial farm with 150,000 broilers
- o No clinical signs or abnormal mortality

Map with a blue point and the labels Belgium and Luxembourg.

<!-- image -->

## Outbreaks of HPAI H5N1 in Poultry Weelde

- o Weelde: Province of Antwerpen
- o Commercial farm with 36,102 breeders
- o Confirmation outbreak: 25 th October 2025
- o Increase mortality
- o Source : most likely wild birds
- o 2 nd farm on the same street and belonged to the same owner
- o Preventive culling and sampling -> HPAI Negative
- o Commercial farm with 35,774 breeders
- o No clinical signs or abnormal mortality

<!-- image -->

[Map with different locations and a location labeled "Belgique"].

<!-- image -->

## Outbreaks of HPAI H5N1 in Poultry Dilsen-Stokkem

- o Dilsen-Stokkem: Province of Limburg
- o Commercial farm with 58,038 laying hens
- o Confirmation outbreak: 20 th of November 2025
- o Mortality with no clinical signs
- o Source : most likely wild birds
- o 2 nd farm on the same street and belonged to the son of the owner
- o Preventive culling and sampling -> HPAI Negative
- o Commercial farm with 59,203 laying hens
- o No clinical signs or abnormal mortality

<!-- image -->

<!-- image -->

## Outbreaks of HPAI H5N1 in Poultry Kinrooi

- o Kinrooi : Province of Limburg
- o Commercial farm with 28.014 rearing laying hens
- o Confirmation outbreak: 22 nd of November 2025
- o Slight mortality with no clinical signs
- o Source : most likely wild birds

<!-- image -->

A map of Belgium with a pin point labeled Essen.

<!-- image -->

<!-- image -->

## Outbreaks of HPAI H5N1 in Poultry Pelt 1 and 2

- o Pelt  : Province of Limburg
- o Commercial farm with 49.781 laying hens
- o Confirmation outbreak: 23 rd of November 2025
- o Mortality with respiratory clinical signs
- o Source : most likely wild birds
- o 2 nd farm on the same street
- o Preventive culling and sampling -> Confirmation of the 2 nd HPAI H5N1 outbreak on the 27 th November 2025
- o Commercial farm with 121,500 broilers
- o No clinical signs or abnormal mortality
- o Source : most likely spill over from the 1 st outbreak in Pelt

<!-- image -->

## Outbreaks of HPAI H5N1 in Poultry Gembloux

- o
- Gembloux : Province of Namur
- o Commercial farm with 11.600 organic broilers
- o Confirmation outbreak: 23 rd of November 2025
- o Mortality with no clinical signs
- o Source : epidemiological investigation is ongoing but most likely wild birds

<!-- image -->

A map of Belgium and Luxembourg is shown.

## Outbreaks of HPAI H5N1 in Poultry La Bruyère 1 and 2

- o La Bruyère : Province of Namur
- o 1 st outbreak:
- o Commercial farm with 6.705 breeders
- o Confirmation outbreak: 23 rd of November 2025
- o Mortality and petechiae on the trachea at the autopsy
- o Source : epidemiological investigation is ongoing but most likely wild birds

## o 2 nd outbreak:

- o Commercial farm with 565 meat ducks
- o Confirmation outbreak: 25 th of November 2025
- o Slight mortality and torticollis
- o Source : epidemiological investigation is ongoing but most likely wild birds

<!-- image -->

[Image Description]

## Outbreaks of HPAI H5N1 Measures in captive birds and Poultry

- o Culling of all captive birds and poultry in all the outbreaks
- o Litter removed, cleaning and disinfection
- o Protocol for restocking with at least a 3-week sanitary vacuum
-  Repopulation after approval by the FASFC
- o Establishment of Protection (3 km) and Surveillance (10 km) zones around all outbreaks in poultry
-  Overlap between the zones
-  in Dilsen-Stokkem (BE), Kinrooi (BE) and Weert (NL)
-  In Gembloux, La Bruyère 1 and 2
-  Part of the 10 km zone in Dilsen-Stokkem, Kinrooi and Pelt on territory of the Netherlands

<!-- image -->

Dilsen-Stokkem, Kinrooi and Weert

<!-- image -->

<!-- image -->

<!-- image -->

<!-- image -->

## Outbreaks of HPAI H5N1 Measures in captive birds and Poultry

- o For the 2 outbreaks in captive birds: no zoning and measures only applied in the outbreak. Based on risk assessment:
-  Type / size of the establishment (hobby holder, < 50 captive birds)
-  Localisation (isolated hobby holding without contacts)
-  Increased risk period still implemented in Belgium
-  Intensive surveillance still in force in Belgium

<!-- image -->

<!-- image -->

## Outbreak of HPAI H5N1 in poultry Additional measures in the whole territory - Increased risk period

- o Increased risk period declared on 15 November 2021: measures adapted following the epidemiological situation -> measures reinforced on the 23 rd October 2025 :
-  Mandatory confinement or protection of all poultry and captive birds of registered holdings
-  Mandatory drinking and feeding inside or protected for poultry and captive birds
-  Prohibition of watering of poultry and captive birds with surface water or rainwater
- o Increased daily biosecurity
- o Increased vigilance
- o Gatherings of poultry and captive birds already forbidden except for hobby poultry noncommercial gatherings

<!-- image -->

<!-- image -->

## Outbreak of HPAI in wild birds 1/10/2025 until 8/12/2025

o 152 HPAI H5 cases  139 H5N1 HPAI  13 H5NX HPAI o 84 different sites
